prestazioni: Engine swap to a 4G63T (from Mitsubishi Lancer Evolution III/IV) with a forged internals build including Eagle connecting rods, JE 87mm forged pistons (8.5:1 compression), and ACL Race Series bearings. Pair with a Garrett GT3076R turbocharger (0.82 A/R turbine housing) and a Tomei 260° camshaft kit for 350-400whp potential on E85 fuel.

Full custom 3-inch stainless steel exhaust system by HKS Hi-Power with a 4-2-1 header design (equal-length primaries) and a custom 3.5-inch straight-pipe section terminating in a GReddy SP2 muffler for optimal spool and top-end power.

Haltech Elite 2500 standalone ECU with a custom wiring harness by Wireworks Racing, integrated with a Link G4+ Monsoon for dual-ECU redundancy. Includes a custom flex-fuel sensor setup (Continental 13577379) and a 4-bar MAP sensor (Honeywell PX3AN2BS4PAAXT) for boost control up to 32 psi.
estetica: Rocket Bunny V1 Freeca-specific widebody kit (front fenders, rear quarter panels, and rear bumper) with custom carbon fiber overfenders by Seibon, retaining the OEM pop-up headlights but adding Hella H4 100W bulbs with custom C-West headlight covers.

Custom 3-piece Work Meister S1 3P 17x9 +35 (front) and 17x10 +40 (rear) wheels with 225/45/17 and 255/40/17 Yokohama Advan A052 tires, paired with BC Racing BR Type-Coilovers (Mitsubishi Freeca-specific valving) and Cusco front strut bar (part #601 390 A).

Full custom interior by Bride with Zeta IV reclinable bucket seats (Mitsubishi Freeca-specific mounting brackets), a Nardi Classic 350mm steering wheel (with a Works Bell quick-release hub), and a custom roll cage by AutoPower (8-point bolt-in, TIG-welded 1.75x0.120 DOM tubing) painted in Mitsubishi Racing Green (code 42K).
